rationale: The citing paper uses Liu, McFerran, and Haws (2020) in its theory-building sections to support the vertical-versus-horizontal (ordinal-versus-nominal) attribute distinction as relevant for product and brand differentiation. It also cites the paper for a specific conceptual claim that ordinal, quality-comparable attributes facilitate social signaling, which the authors use to justify why relevance aligns with vertical differentiation. Although the citation is not method-reuse or a direct extension, it plays a meaningful framing role rather than a passing mention.
relation_description: The citing paper draws on Liu et al. (2020) as prior conceptual evidence that attribute structure (ordinal vs. nominal) matters for comparison and signaling. This supports and underpins the citing paper's broader vertical/horizontal brand differentiation framework in the labor-market context.
